explanation: This formula expresses that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must hold during that time. In other words, prop_1 is required to be true throughout every period in which prop_0 is true. This is captured by the formula G (prop_0 -> prop_1), meaning: globally, if prop_0 then prop_1.

The statement "During the prop_0 the prop_1" means that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' must also hold at the same time. In LTL, this is captured by the implication 'if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 holds', enforced globally. Thus, the translation is G(prop_0 -> prop_1).

The requirement states that whenever 'prop_0' holds, then 'prop_1' must also hold during the same time. In LTL, this is typically captured by stating that globally, if 'prop_0' is true, then 'prop_1' must also be true at that time. This is the 'globally' (G) operator and an implication.

The expression 'during the prop_0, the prop_1' means that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at the same time. In LTL, this can be captured as 'globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must hold as well'.
The formula captures that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 also holds at the same time. This is expressed by stating that globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must be true as well.
This states that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must hold during the same time. So, globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true.

The phrase 'during the self test mode the prop_0' suggests that whenever the system is in self test mode (though a proposition for 'self test mode' is not among the atomic propositions), the atomic proposition 'prop_0' should hold. But since only 'prop_0' is given, it can be interpreted as 'prop_0' should be true throughout the self test mode, or simply 'prop_0' must always hold if the context of 'self test mode' is implicit. Therefore, the formula ensures that 'prop_0' always holds globally.
The phrase means: whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at the same time. In other words, prop_1 must hold throughout every period where prop_0 is true.
The phrase 'During the prop_0 the prop_1' can be interpreted as: whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 also holds at the same time. In other words, at all times, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 is also true.

This specification requires that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold for the entire duration that prop_0 holds. In LTL, this is captured by saying globally, if prop_0 holds, then, until prop_0 stops holding, prop_1 must be true.
